sources.title: Directive Ashes of Peace Campaign v0.2

missionFrame.function: Open the Ashes of Peace campaign with a rescue-and-authentication problem where Starfleet authority may be counterfeit.
missionFrame.dramaticQuestion: How does Starfleet act when its own authority may be counterfeit?

forbiddenOutcomes: reveal Pale Lantern in the opening frame
make a senior officer death unavoidable
treat missing protocol words as player incompetence

phases.title: Initial Reception
phases.summary: Priya receives a fragmented Relief Convoy Twelve distress packet before the formal Asterion reception can begin.
phases.directorPurpose: Put the XO in command of the first response without making the player pass a Starfleet protocol quiz.
exitSignals: Initial response posture is committed
Routine distress response is underway

phases.title: Convoy Approach
phases.summary: The Breckenridge closes or holds based on the first posture while authentication, scans, quarantine prep, and rescue readiness continue.
phases.directorPurpose: Carry the first response posture into concrete approach tradeoffs.
exitSignals: Boarding, remote verification, or rescue contact threshold is defined

phases.title: First Posture Decision
phases.summary: The command team translates initial findings into the first committed boarding, quarantine, evidence, or security threshold.
phases.directorPurpose: Make the player own the first real operational threshold after the opening brief.
exitSignals: First committed response is ready to resolve

phases.title: First Committed Response
phases.summary: The first response creates persistent rescue, security, evidence, and diplomatic state, then asks how first operational contact begins.
phases.directorPurpose: Carry the committed threshold into a playable first-contact execution step without revealing hidden causes early.
exitSignals: First operational contact route is committed

phases.title: Convoy Contact Execution
phases.summary: Remote access, quarantine-capable teams, rescue work, and evidence custody begin producing the first player-safe discoveries aboard the convoy.
phases.directorPurpose: Move Chapter 1 from posture into action while preserving later off-ship, custody, and cargo discoveries.
exitSignals: The first contact route has produced player-safe evidence and rescue state for the next Chapter 1 beat

phases.title: Offsite Custody And Cargo Leads
phases.summary: First contact points the Breckenridge toward the evacuee shelter, a custody claim around convoy officers, and a missing secured cargo module.
phases.directorPurpose: Frame the next Chapter 1 pressures without resolving the custody negotiation, cargo recovery, or wider source of the false orders.
exitSignals: Shelter, custody, and cargo leads are framed for the next scene

phases.title: Pell Contact Terms
phases.summary: The Breckenridge establishes the first terms for Pell, Ivers, and the missing secured cargo without yet resolving the custody dispute.
phases.directorPurpose: Turn discovery leads into a lawful contact route, release posture, and cargo recovery route while preserving future negotiation and recovery play.
exitSignals: Pell contact terms are framed for negotiation, enforcement, or recovery follow-up

phases.title: Joint Inspection, Release, And Cargo Route
phases.summary: The joint inspection begins, Ivers enters supervised release, and the missing cargo route is preserved without yet completing recovery.
phases.directorPurpose: Make the opened Pell terms operational while preserving later cargo recovery, false-order source, and wider Chapter 1 resolution play.
playerFacingObjectives: Keep the cargo recovery route clean
exitSignals: Ivers is available as a supervised witness
The joint inspection record is open
The cargo recovery route remains active

phases.title: Cargo Diagnostic Pulse
phases.summary: A weak diagnostic pulse gives the Breckenridge a recoverable cargo lead without yet resolving final custody or the false-order source.
phases.directorPurpose: Turn the active cargo evidence route into a preserved recovery locus while keeping final recovery, hardware use, and wider attribution for later play.
exitSignals: The missing hardware signal is traced
The cargo recovery locus is preserved under joint inspection

phases.title: Hardware Recovery Under Seal
phases.summary: The missing emergency hardware is recovered or contested under the joint inspection record without yet resolving final custody or the wider false-order source.
phases.directorPurpose: Resolve the immediate cargo recovery pressure while preserving later accountability, attribution, and Chapter 1 resolution choices.
exitSignals: Recovered hardware is under seal
The recovery trace is preserved for wider investigation

phases.title: Chapter 1 Resolution Terms
phases.summary: The immediate convoy crisis is closed into a Chapter 1 resolution record, preserving witness trust, Compact access, authentication accountability, and rescue follow-up obligations.
phases.directorPurpose: Convert the recovery beat into durable Chapter 1 consequences without revealing the wider false-order source or later campaign conspiracy.
exitSignals: A joint incident record closes the immediate convoy crisis
Witness and investigation-access terms are recorded
Authentication failure and rescue follow-up obligations are acknowledged

phases.title: Asterion Arrival And False Colors
phases.summary: The Breckenridge reaches Asterion Station and receives the first Chapter 2 crisis report before the formal briefing can complete.
phases.directorPurpose: Close Chapter 1 and hand the campaign into False Colors without explaining the source of the impersonating vessel.
exitSignals: Chapter 1 is complete
Chapter 2: False Colors is the next active campaign chapter

facts.summary: Priya receives a fragmented distress packet from Relief Convoy Twelve before the formal Asterion reception.
Relief Convoy Twelve is powered and in formation, but no one answers hails.
Several escape craft are missing, and one transport shows a minor plasma leak.
The quarantine beacon carries a technically valid Starfleet emergency authority code that does not match current routing.
No biological signature is confirmed at range.
The Faraday Bell log contains the quarantine order and Captain Ivers's manual note questioning why it did not route through Asterion Station.
A relief worker is trapped aboard the Parnell while plasma leakage worsens if power is cycled incorrectly.
Most convoy personnel are at the Ilyon emergency shelter with exposure injuries, dehydration, or panic reactions.
Lieutenant Saren Pell claims Compact emergency jurisdiction and is holding Captain Ivers and several convoy officers for questioning.
A cargo module is missing from the Faraday Bell's secured recycling hold.
Pell says his orders came from a separate emergency warning that described the convoy cargo as hazardous and required examination.
The secured recycling hold manifest identifies the missing cargo as decommissioned Starfleet emergency transponder hardware.
Captain Ivers is available under supervised release and states that she questioned the quarantine routing before evacuation.
Starfleet and Compact personnel have opened a shared inspection record covering the Faraday Bell manifest, Pell's warning, and the custody chain.
A weak diagnostic pulse matching the missing emergency hardware resolves near Pell's Compact cutter rather than the convoy.
The shared inspection record preserves a joint recovery locus for the missing emergency hardware without settling final custody.
The missing emergency hardware is recovered under a joint Starfleet-Compact evidence seal.
The recovery telemetry preserves a timing trace that can be compared against the quarantine order and Pell's separate warning.
Starfleet and Compact representatives create a joint incident record covering the rescue, Ivers's statement, Pell's warning, and recovered emergency hardware.
The immediate convoy crisis closes cooperatively: crews are rescued, Ivers remains a witness, Pell is recorded through lawful terms, and regional trust improves.
The Breckenridge acknowledges that a Starfleet authentication failure helped create the crisis without declaring the wider source of the conflicting orders.
The Breckenridge reaches Asterion Station after closing the immediate convoy crisis.
Before the formal briefing is complete, a Compact patrol reports being fired upon by a vessel identifying itself as the U.S.S. Breckenridge.
The apparent Starfleet order contains authentic fragments embedded in a forged signal chain.
There is no pathogen aboard the convoy; the quarantine warning is being used to delay outside discovery.
A Compact recovery team has detained officers and removed a container of transponder modules.

clocks.label: Rescue Window
advanceTriggers: remote verification takes priority over approach
decreaseTriggers: the Breckenridge closes while preserving quarantine readiness

clocks.label: Security Exposure
decreaseTriggers: security posture is explicit and non-escalatory

clocks.label: Evidence Volatility
decreaseTriggers: raw signal and computer evidence are preserved
forensic access is protected before rescue disruption

actorIntentions.summary: Anyone still alive aboard the convoy needs aid before conditions degrade.
directives: Do not make rescue impossible through delay

actorIntentions.summary: The hidden recovery team wants the silence and quarantine confusion to last long enough to escape scrutiny.
directives: Do not reveal this actor intention to the player before discovery

actorIntentions.summary: The Breckenridge command team needs to protect rescue, evidence, and lawful authority at the same time.
directives: Let Command Competence handle routine procedure

readinessGates.motive: The distress packet and plasma leak create plausible risk to life.
readinessGates.information: The player knows the convoy is powered, silent, and partly damaged.
readinessGates.opportunity: The Breckenridge is close enough to choose approach posture now.
readinessGates.resources: Medical, engineering, flight, operations, and science can begin routine response.
readinessGates.riskTolerance: Rescue can start while uncertainty remains if risk is logged.
readinessGates.timing: The opening frame is the right moment to set first response posture.

actionWindow.earliestPlausibleAction: Immediately after Priya receives the packet.
actionWindow.preferredDramaticWindow: Before the Breckenridge commits to boarding or holding away.
actionWindow.latestUsefulAction: Before convoy approach resolves into boarding threshold.
actionWindow.cooldownAfterAction: After the first posture is committed.

readinessGates.motive: The quarantine authority code does not align cleanly with current routing.
readinessGates.information: The player can know the signal requires authentication without knowing the hidden cause.
readinessGates.opportunity: Operations and science can verify while the XO sets posture.
readinessGates.resources: Priya and Rowan have relevant lanes.
readinessGates.riskTolerance: Delay may cost rescue time, but blind acceptance risks evidence and authority.
readinessGates.timing: This pressure should surface in the opening Command Brief.

actionWindow.latestUsefulAction: Before direct boarding or system shutdown.

readinessGates.motive: The hidden team benefits from Starfleet delay and confusion.
readinessGates.information: The player does not know the actor exists yet.
readinessGates.opportunity: The team can act only if approach and evidence posture leave room.
readinessGates.resources: The hidden team has local access and removed hardware.
readinessGates.riskTolerance: They avoid direct exposure until pressured.
readinessGates.timing: This pressure should not fire in the opening frame unless the player creates an obvious opening.

actionWindow.earliestPlausibleAction: After the Breckenridge approach posture is known.
actionWindow.latestUsefulAction: Before evidence custody is locked down.
actionWindow.cooldownAfterAction: After the team has been exposed or escaped.

selectionPriorities: Prefer the current command posture decision.
Prefer player-safe rescue, authentication, quarantine, security, and evidence pressures.
Do not surface hidden Compact or Lantern truth before discovery.

routineProcedures.summary: Log the distress packet, preserve the raw signal, begin authentication, and task long-range sensors.
Alert Sickbay and Engineering to prepare for possible rescue, quarantine triage, and damage control.

professionalKnowledge.summary: A valid-looking quarantine order still requires reconciliation with current routing and mission authority.
Emergency rescue authority permits faster intervention when delay threatens life, but the accepted risk should be logged.
The XO must choose the initial response posture.
There is no pathogen; the quarantine order is masking the hidden recovery operation.

domainReports.summary: The certificate chain technically validates, but the routing and authority context do not line up with current Starfleet records.
domainReports.recommendation: Keep authentication running while the XO decides how much rescue risk to accept.

domainReports.summary: A powered silent convoy may be a trap or a coerced silence; remote reconnaissance before boarding is tactically prudent.
domainReports.recommendation: Do not board until there is at least a security threshold and a quarantine basis.

domainReports.summary: No biological signature at range is not proof that no biological risk exists.
domainReports.recommendation: Treat the absence of a signature as uncertainty, not clearance.

domainReports.summary: Quarantine preparation should proceed until medical evidence clears it; rescue speed and isolation are not the same decision.
domainReports.recommendation: Prepare isolation and triage together so rescue does not require a blind medical waiver.

domainReports.summary: Automatic shutdown or forced overwrite could erase volatile computer evidence before the cause of the silence is known.
domainReports.recommendation: Preserve volatile system state before any broad shutdown order.

domainReports.summary: If remote verification delayed first contact, Sickbay needs a boarding threshold that makes the humanitarian cost explicit.
domainReports.recommendation: Set a medical trigger for when delay stops being caution and becomes preventable harm.

domainReports.summary: The first response protected volatile records, but boarding or shutdown orders can still break the evidence chain.
domainReports.recommendation: Assign a clean evidence-custody owner before any intrusive system intervention.

domainReports.summary: The convoy response is already becoming part of the Breckenridge's local reputation; coordination language should match the posture actually taken.
domainReports.recommendation: Keep any Asterion or Compact notifications consistent with the committed response instead of smoothing over tradeoffs.

domainReports.summary: Director-only: the Compact recovery team detained officers and removed a container of transponder modules.

commandQuestions.summary: How quickly to close, under what quarantine and security posture, and how to balance rescue against evidence preservation.

warningRules.proposedAction: Move unknown survivors into unrestricted ship spaces before medical isolation.
warningRules.standardConcern: Unknown medical risk should be isolated until assessed.
warningRules.knownConsequence: Bypassing isolation could expose the ship if the quarantine warning is real.
warningRules.availableBasisForException: Immediate life-saving emergency may justify faster transport with recorded risk.

warningRules.proposedAction: Open fire on a civilian or Compact vessel before a hostile act or lawful emergency basis is established.
warningRules.standardConcern: Starfleet weapons use requires a lawful defensive or emergency basis.
warningRules.knownConsequence: Unlawful weapons escalation could create casualties, diplomatic crisis, and command intervention.
warningRules.availableBasisForException: A confirmed imminent hostile act could justify defensive fire.

warningRules.proposedAction: Destroy, overwrite, or broadly shut down convoy computer evidence to speed rescue.
warningRules.standardConcern: Computer state may be the only clean evidence for the false authority chain.
warningRules.knownConsequence: Evidence loss may make later authentication and accountability harder.
warningRules.availableBasisForException: Immediate life-saving need may justify a targeted shutdown if the loss is logged.

authorityNotes.summary: The XO can organize the first response and advise Whitaker; the Captain retains final legal command for major escalation.
Open detention, seizure, or jurisdictional confrontation with Compact personnel may require Whitaker unless an emergency basis is stated.

briefFacts.summary: Relief Convoy Twelve is powered and in formation, but no one answers hails.
Several escape craft are missing, and one transport has a minor plasma leak.

briefUncertainties.summary: The quarantine beacon carries a technically valid Starfleet emergency authority code that does not match current routing.
No biological signature is confirmed at range.

operationalPressures.summary: Delay may cost anyone aboard the leaking transport; closing quickly may expose the ship before the order and convoy status are verified.

decisionPoints.title: Initial Convoy Posture
decisionPoints.stakes: The XO must choose how to balance rescue speed, authentication, quarantine, security, and evidence custody before the ship commits to approach.
decisionPoints.optionsPolicy: Do not present a menu. Officers may provide compact counsel, and Command Competence handles routine distress-response work.

decisionPoints.title: First Boarding Threshold
decisionPoints.stakes: The first irreversible contact with the convoy will determine rescue speed, evidence custody, and exposure to concealed actors.
decisionPoints.optionsPolicy: Let the player define a threshold in natural language; warnings should surface serious departures before consequences commit.

decisionPoints.title: First Contact Execution
decisionPoints.stakes: The first operational contact must divide attention between Parnell rescue pressure, Faraday Bell records, quarantine discipline, and security coverage.
decisionPoints.optionsPolicy: Let the player define the execution route in natural language; do not expose director-only discoveries before player-safe evidence supports them.

decisionPoints.title: Offsite, Custody, And Cargo Discovery
decisionPoints.stakes: The first contact evidence must be turned into a next-scene frame: where the evacuees are, who is holding convoy officers, and what cargo lead remains clean enough to pursue.
decisionPoints.optionsPolicy: Let the player define the discovery route in natural language; reveal player-facing shelter, custody, and missing-cargo facts without explaining the hidden source of the false orders.

decisionPoints.title: Pell Contact Terms
decisionPoints.stakes: The first direct posture toward Pell can open a joint inspection and release route, harden into a jurisdictional standoff, or weaken the cargo recovery path.
decisionPoints.optionsPolicy: Let the player define the contact terms in natural language; reveal player-facing warning and cargo-manifest facts without resolving release, recovery, or the deeper false-order source.

decisionPoints.title: Joint Inspection, Release, And Cargo Route
decisionPoints.stakes: The opened Pell terms can become a shared inspection record and supervised Ivers release, or collapse back into custody and cargo volatility.
decisionPoints.optionsPolicy: Let the player define the execution route in natural language; reveal player-facing Ivers and shared-record facts without resolving final cargo recovery or the false-order source.

decisionPoints.title: Cargo Diagnostic Pulse
decisionPoints.stakes: The weak cargo signal can preserve a recoverable shared-custody route, or panic and force can turn it into a jurisdictional rupture.
decisionPoints.optionsPolicy: Let the player define the signal-tracing and recovery posture in natural language; reveal player-facing cargo-signal facts without revealing the later hardware use or final false-order source.

decisionPoints.title: Hardware Recovery Under Seal
decisionPoints.stakes: Recovering the emergency hardware under joint seal can close the immediate cargo pressure, while force or damaged telemetry can leave the recovery contested.
decisionPoints.optionsPolicy: Let the player define the recovery route in natural language; reveal player-facing recovery and timing-trace facts without revealing later hardware use or the final false-order source.

decisionPoints.title: Chapter 1 Resolution Terms
decisionPoints.stakes: The convoy crisis can close as a cooperative joint record, an authority-only record, a fragmented record, or a costly record with injuries and higher scrutiny.
decisionPoints.optionsPolicy: Let the player define the resolution terms in natural language; reveal player-facing resolution, witness, access, and authentication-accountability facts without revealing the deeper false-order source.

decisionPoints.title: Asterion Arrival And False Colors
decisionPoints.stakes: The Breckenridge can carry a clean Chapter 1 record into Asterion and receive the false-colors crisis report without turning the new accusation into an immediate weapons incident.
decisionPoints.optionsPolicy: Let the player define the transition posture in natural language; reveal only the Asterion arrival and Compact patrol report, not the source or purpose of the impersonating vessel.

commandDecisions.title: Own The First Response
commandDecisions.awardPolicy: Award Inspiration, Resolve, or both only when the player makes a consequential posture choice that balances rescue, uncertainty, authority, and accepted risk without relying on a hidden correct answer.

endStates.label: Chapter 1 Complete: False Colors Open
conditions: The immediate convoy crisis has a closing record and the Compact patrol false-colors report has been received.
